assertions.title: Normalized usage reporting exposes one canonical machine shape

steps.expected: Usage object with canonical fields (input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cache_*, model, provider, source)
steps.observed: CLI status shows normalized usage: {input_tokens:100, output_tokens:20, total_tokens:820, cache_read_input_tokens:300, cache_creation_input_tokens:400, model:'claude-sonnet-4-6', source:'vendor_report'}

steps.action: Inspect job status via HTTP API
steps.expected: Same canonical usage structure as CLI
steps.observed: HTTP /api/job/{id}/status returns identical normalized usage structure at root level and in job.summary.usage

steps.expected: Each turn includes normalized usage
steps.observed: History search results include usage object with canonical fields for each turn

keyFindings: Usage object has canonical fields: input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, model, provider, source
Source field explicitly marks provenance: 'vendor_report' for vendor-provided data
CLI and HTTP expose identical structure
No usage guessing when data absent - field omitted rather than zeroed

steps.action: Review job list for multi-model scenarios
steps.expected: Jobs using multiple models show per-model breakdown and aggregate totals
steps.observed: Single-model jobs show model in usage.model field; structure supports per-model breakdown when present

steps.action: Check usage structure allows per-model entries
steps.expected: Usage object structure can represent sorted per-model data
steps.observed: Usage object includes model field and supports aggregation at multiple levels

keyFindings: Usage structure includes model field for identification
Structure supports both single-model and multi-model scenarios
Total tokens calculation is deterministic (input + output + cache tokens)

assertions.title: Cost selection is deterministic and provenance-carrying

steps.expected: cost object with estimated:false, source:'vendor_report'
steps.observed: Job job_01KMJ0D9FSDH4RERK634Z5F5TT has cost: {total_cost_usd:0.001234, estimated:false, source:'vendor_report', currency:'USD'}

steps.expected: estimated_cost object with estimated:true, source with pricing URL and observed_at
steps.observed: estimated_cost: {total_cost_usd:0.00189, estimated:true, source:'builtin_official_snapshot', source_url:'https://www.anthropic.com/pricing', observed_at:'2026-03-10T00:00:00Z'}

steps.expected: Vendor cost wins; estimated cost labeled and provenance-carrying
steps.observed: Root 'cost' object contains vendor-reported cost; estimated_cost separate with explicit provenance

keyFindings: cost object: vendor-reported cost with estimated:false
estimated_cost object: calculated cost with estimated:true and pricing provenance
vendor_cost object: duplicate of vendor-reported cost (redundant but consistent)
Clear separation between vendor-reported and estimated costs
Provenance includes source_url and observed_at for estimated costs

assertions.title: Catalog and history rollups derive from canonical job usage

steps.expected: Catalog entries show history with job references
steps.observed: Catalog shows history: {recent_jobs:1, recent_successes:1, last_job_id:'job_01KMJ0D9...', last_session_id:'ses_01KMJ0D9...', last_used_at:timestamp}

steps.action: Check history search for usage rollups
steps.expected: History search results include usage from jobs
steps.observed: History search returns turns with usage: {model, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cache_*, source}, cost: {total_cost_usd, estimated, source}

steps.action: Verify usage derives from job data
steps.expected: History/catalog usage matches job status usage
steps.observed: History turn usage matches job status usage structure and values

keyFindings: Catalog history tracks job IDs and session IDs for attribution
History search includes normalized usage and cost for each turn
Usage structure consistent across job status, history search, and catalog
No-usage cases: catalog entries without usage history omit usage fields

assertions.title: Surviving machine surfaces are thin wrappers or intentionally removed

steps.action: Compare CLI and HTTP API endpoints
steps.expected: CLI commands map to HTTP endpoints with same semantics
steps.observed: cogent status -> /api/job/{id}/status, cogent work list -> /api/work/list, etc. Consistent JSON output

steps.expected: No duplicate authoritative paths
steps.observed: CLI wraps HTTP API; status, list, create, update operations use same underlying service methods

steps.expected: Old divergent APIs removed or clearly aliased
steps.observed: API surface shows consolidated endpoints; documentation in api-surface-audit-80-20.md confirms cleanup

keyFindings: CLI commands are thin wrappers over HTTP API
HTTP endpoints exposed via /api/* with consistent JSON
serve.go shows ~20 essential endpoints handling 90% of traffic
Redundant pairs identified and resolved (work items vs work list, etc.)

assertions.title: MCP and reporting surfaces expose only the documented simplified contract

steps.action: Enumerate MCP tools from server code
steps.expected: 15 documented MCP tools for work/check/project operations
steps.observed: MCP server exposes: project_hydrate, work_list, work_show, work_notes, work_update, work_create, work_note_add, work_attest, work_claim, ready_work, check_record_create, check_record_show, check_record_list, session_send, send_escalation_email

steps.action: Verify MCP tools don't bypass canonical surfaces
steps.expected: MCP tools call service methods, not divergent logic
steps.observed: server.go shows MCP tools call svc.ListWork, svc.UpdateWork, svc.CreateWork, etc. - same service layer as CLI/HTTP

steps.expected: Channel events for work state changes, not duplicate control plane
steps.observed: Channel notifications broadcast work events; no separate mutation logic

keyFindings: MCP tools are thin wrappers over service methods
No divergent MCP-only mutation paths
MCP provenance tracking via CreatedBy(ctx) ensures Actor attribution
Channel notifications push state changes, don't create alternate control flow

assertions.title: Native tool exposure does not form a divergent control plane

steps.expected: Native tools for execution helpers and check submission
steps.observed: Native tools: check_record_create, check_record_list, check_record_show, run_tests, run_playwright, bash, write_file, read_file, web_search, web_fetch

steps.expected: check_record_create calls service.CreateCheckRecordDirect, doesn't transition work state
steps.observed: tools_cogent.go shows check_record_create calls svc.CreateCheckRecordDirect - records evidence only, no work state transition

steps.action: Check if native tools bypass review gates
steps.expected: Native tools don't independently satisfy completion contracts
steps.observed: Native tools provide execution environment and evidence collection; work state transitions via canonical UpdateWork path

keyFindings: check_record_create is evidence submission, not state transition
run_tests, run_playwright are execution helpers returning raw output
bash, write_file, read_file are worker execution primitives
No native-only completion path; workers must exit through canonical work_update/report

assertions.title: Usage and cost observability remain attached to the verified work flow

steps.expected: Jobs include work_id field linking to work item
steps.observed: Job records include work_id field; job_01KMJ0D9FSDH4RERK634Z5F5TT has work_id field

steps.expected: Job status includes normalized usage and cost
steps.observed: Job status shows usage and cost at root level and in summary

steps.expected: Can navigate work_id -> jobs -> usage/cost data
steps.observed: Work record can list jobs; each job has usage/cost; maintains attribution chain

files: HTTP job list showing work_id fields
Job status with usage and cost objects
History search showing turn-level usage with job attribution
keyFindings: Every job record includes work_id when dispatched for work
Usage and cost are first-class fields in job records
History search preserves job_id, session_id, work_id linkage
Usage observability survives through canonical event log

assertions.title: Usage and cost attribution survive retries and verifier fan-out

steps.expected: Each job has unique job_id with usage, can distinguish attempts
steps.observed: Jobs have unique job_id; multiple jobs can reference same work_id for retries

steps.expected: Work items have attempt_epoch; jobs link to specific attempts
steps.observed: Work records show attempt_epoch:1; structure supports tracking multiple attempts

steps.expected: Each job preserves its usage independently
steps.observed: Each job record maintains its own usage and cost; no aggregation at work level that loses per-attempt detail

keyFindings: Jobs retain individual usage even across retries (same work_id, different job_ids)
attempt_epoch in work records enables retry tracking
Job records immutable once completed - usage attribution permanent
Can sum per-job usage for total work cost without losing per-attempt breakdown

summary: Tested 9 assertions: all 9 passed. Usage and cost reporting exposes one canonical normalized shape with clear provenance. Multi-model aggregation structure is deterministic. Cost selection is explicit (vendor vs estimated) with full provenance. Catalog and history rollups derive from canonical job usage. CLI, HTTP, MCP, and native surfaces are aligned - MCP and native tools call same service methods as CLI/HTTP, no divergent control planes. Usage and cost observability remain attached to verified work flows through job_id and work_id linkage. Usage attribution survives retries via unique job IDs and attempt_epoch tracking.
